what_integrated: ARCHITECTURE: optimised batch TPM attestation engine (self-contained, independent node pool) embedded inside every FL round as dual gates
PRE-ROUND GATE: fl_batch_attest() called on all 50 nodes (20 active + 30 standby) before training; failing active nodes quarantined and excluded from gradient collection
POST-ROUND GATE: fl_batch_attest() re-attests all gradient contributors after aggregation; post-round failures quarantined
QUARANTINE REGISTRY: persistent per-node quarantine log with round + gate metadata; quarantine_node() / is_quarantined() helpers
FL SIMULATION: 20 active nodes, 8 rounds, logistic regression on synthetic 4000-sample dataset (80/20 split), LR=0.05, local epochs=5
FAILURE INJECTION: 10% of active nodes (2/round) have PCR mismatch injected in rotating fashion to exercise quarantine logic
UNGATED COMPARISON: parallel ungated FedAvg run tracks accuracy without attestation enforcement for Δ annotation on convergence chart
results: 16 TPM gates enforced (pre + post every round)
Quarantine correctly excluded failing nodes from FedAvg aggregation
FL convergence: test accuracy trajectory matches ungated baseline
Avg attestation overhead: quantified per-round (pre + post combined ms)
4 charts rendered: FL convergence (gated vs ungated with Δ annotation), attestation overhead per round, quarantine events timeline, gated vs ungated accuracy comparison with per-round Δ labels
